title: Error: Formpic conversion to Struct has detected an error
breadcrumbs: Error: Formpic conversion to Struct has detected an error
text: Error: Formpic conversion to Struct has detected an error This message is generated when there is a mismatch between the way a relationship is defined in Form layout and the way it is defined in the component structure. Problem In some situations in Uniface 9 (and earlier versions), it is possible to create components with ambiguous layout definitions. The ambiguity is concerns two entities, ENT1 and ENT2, which are drawn in such a way that ENT2 is logically inside ENT1, while in the component's structure (FORMPIC property), entity ENT2 occurs before entity ENT1. In Uniface 10, it is not possible to edit such an ambiguous Form, so it is removed during migration. Sample Message Migrating component MYCOMPONENT === MIGRATION ERROR [cpt:MYCOMPONENT] === Failed to migrate cpt:MYCOMPONENT . Object will be removed from the repository. ErrorID: -100006 Error : Formpic conversion to struct has detected an error Context information: [context] [Error 1] = "The logical relation between Entity ENT1.MODEL and Entity ENT2.MODEL differs from their painted relation" [Reported error] = "-10106" [status] = "-1" Copy Resolution To fix the problem, open the component in Uniface 9, and modify the layout before migrating the component again. There is no fixed method for resolving the ambiguity. It depends on the way the frames are defined, and on other frames that may be present in the vicinity. In Uniface 9, the Form Editor does not clearly show that the layout is ambiguous, but the ambiguity can become visible while handling the layout. For example, consider the following layout in the Uniface 9 Form Editor. In this layout, entity ENT2 (which has a border) is logically inside ENT1. However, the frame for entity ENT2 occurs first in Structure. Initially, the structure tree shows these two entities as siblings. In the layout, ENT2 is drawn on top of ENT1 (the yellow background color of ENT2 is not visible): If you try to remove the border of ENT2 by choosing Border > No from its context menu, the change fails with a warning , but the structure tree and layout are suddenly changed, with the yellow background color of ENT2 becoming visible and ENT2 is positioned inside Ent1, and field FLD is moved from entity ENT1 to entity ENT2: If you store the component, exit from the Form Editor, and then open the Form Editor again, the initial structure will be shown again. To fix this, you need to change location or dimensions of ENT2 so that it is clearly drawn inside ENT1. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
